Thanks for opening the discussion. Based on the latest market reference I’m seeing, I can offer supply at 6.75 per unit, which reflects current pricing and gives us a clear starting point for locking in volume. If you’re planning a meaningful commitment, I’m open to discussing structure and delivery terms alongside price. There is no buyer offer to respond to, so the appropriate move is to anchor with an opening ask. The latest market-check shows 6.75, and starting there protects margin while leaving room to negotiate if the buyer pushes back.

I can’t move forward at 6.75 per unit. That level is far outside the range I can support for procurement economics, and it would leave no room for sustainable downstream margin. If you want to continue, you would need to come back with a dramatically lower number that reflects a realistic buy-side range and the possibility of ongoing volume.
